cases.title: Testing-plan session open/close row: check-in rejected before session is Open
preconditions: Class session exists in Scheduled state with enrolled students per testing-plan §3.1 Session open/close coverage block.
Student actor is authenticated with active enrollment in the session class section.
A QR token fixture bound to the session exists (may be pre-issued or synthetic for API path).
steps: While session state is Scheduled, submit POST /v1/check-ins as enrolled student with valid qrToken.
Record HTTP response error.code and persisted CheckInAttempt outcome.
Lecturer opens session via POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/open.
Re-submit POST /v1/check-ins with current valid QR token for the same student.
Query attendance records for the student-session pair.
cases.expected: Pre-open check-in is rejected with SessionNotOpen and a failed CheckInAttempt is persisted; after open, check-in is no longer blocked by BR-01 and may proceed to downstream rules.

cases.title: Check-in service persists failed CheckInAttempt with SessionNotOpen when session is Scheduled
preconditions: Integration harness uses real DB transaction boundaries for class_sessions, check_in_attempts, and attendance_records tables.
A Scheduled session row exists with at least one active enrollment.
Student auth context matches enrolled studentUserId.
steps: Invoke check-in command (POST /v1/check-ins service path) against Scheduled session with valid token binding.
Query check_in_attempts table for the student and session.
Query attendance_records table for the same student-session pair.
Assert session row state remains Scheduled with no openedAt mutation.
cases.expected: Exactly one CheckInAttempt row is inserted with outcome SessionNotOpen and submittedAt timestamp; no AttendanceRecord success row is created; session state remains Scheduled.

cases.title: Non-Open session states reject check-in without illegal state mutation
preconditions: Session fixtures exist in Scheduled, Closed, and Cancelled states with known sessionIds.
Enrolled student accounts and token bindings are available for each fixture.
steps: Record beforeState for Scheduled session and invoke check-in command.
Record afterState and rejection code for Scheduled session.
Repeat check-in attempt against Closed session fixture; capture SessionClosed rejection.
Repeat check-in attempt against Cancelled session fixture; capture SessionNotOpen rejection.
Query persistence after each attempt and confirm session state unchanged.
cases.expected: beforeState equals afterState for all three fixtures (Scheduled, Closed, Cancelled); Scheduled and Cancelled return SessionNotOpen; Closed returns SessionClosed; no attendance success mutation occurs.

cases.title: POST /v1/check-ins on Scheduled session returns 422 SessionNotOpen envelope
preconditions: Enrolled Student is authenticated with Bearer token and Idempotency-Key header.
Target class session is in Scheduled state.
qrToken is non-empty and bound to the target session.
steps: Send POST /v1/check-ins with qrToken, Authorization, and Idempotency-Key headers.
Validate HTTP status code and JSON envelope shape {data, meta, error}.
Assert data is null and error.code is SessionNotOpen.
Assert error.message is localized Vietnamese text explaining attendance is not yet open.
Assert meta includes requestId and timestamp fields.
cases.expected: HTTP 422 rule-failure response with standard envelope; error.code SessionNotOpen; localized message per API design §2.3 and BR-01 outcome.

cases.title: POST /v1/check-ins on Closed session returns 422 SessionClosed envelope
preconditions: Class session was Open and is now Closed with closedAt recorded.
Enrolled Student is authenticated.
qrToken was valid during Open window or synthetic token bound to closed session.
steps: Send POST /v1/check-ins as enrolled Student against the Closed session.
Validate HTTP 422 and envelope fields.
Assert error.code is SessionClosed with Vietnamese message indicating attendance has ended.
Query GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance or DB and confirm no new success attendance row for this attempt.
cases.expected: HTTP 422 with error.code SessionClosed per BR-01/BR-02 distinction; no new successful AttendanceRecord created; failed attempt logged.

cases.title: Cancelled session check-in returns SessionNotOpen not success path
preconditions: Class session fixture exists in Cancelled state.
Enrolled student with valid authentication is available.
qrToken payload references the cancelled session context.
steps: Submit POST /v1/check-ins for the Cancelled session.
Capture HTTP status, error.code, and error.message.
Verify no AttendanceRecord with Present or Late status is created.
Verify CheckInAttempt row exists with SessionNotOpen outcome.
cases.expected: API rejects with SessionNotOpen for Cancelled state; persistence shows failed attempt only; session remains Cancelled.

cases.title: Rule-order short-circuit: SessionNotOpen returned before token, enrollment, and duplicate checks
preconditions: Scheduled session exists (not Open).
Student is not enrolled in the section OR has prior successful attendance OR token is expired—conditions that would fail BR-06, BR-07, or BR-03 if session were Open.
Student actor is authenticated.
steps: Submit POST /v1/check-ins with expired qrToken against Scheduled session.
Assert error.code is SessionNotOpen, not ExpiredQr.
Submit with unenrolled student token against Scheduled session.
Assert error.code is SessionNotOpen, not NotEnrolled.
Submit duplicate-prone payload for student with existing success record against Scheduled session.
Assert error.code is SessionNotOpen, not DuplicateCheckIn.
cases.expected: Validation order per API design §5.1 and validation rules §2.2: session state check (BR-01) short-circuits before BR-03, BR-06, and BR-07 evaluations when session is not Open.

cases.title: Lecturer denied POST /v1/check-ins; Student denied session open control
preconditions: Scheduled session exists in lecturer-assigned section.
Lecturer and Student accounts are authenticated with valid tokens.
Permission matrix §3.1: only Student may submit QR check-in; only Lecturer may open session.
steps: Lecturer calls POST /v1/check-ins with qrToken for the Scheduled session.
Assert HTTP 403 with error.code Forbidden per VR-CI-02.
Student calls POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/open.
Assert HTTP 403 Forbidden or OutOfScope.
Query class_sessions and confirm state remains Scheduled.
Assigned Lecturer calls POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/open as positive control.
cases.expected: Lecturer check-in submission denied with 403 Forbidden; Student session open denied; session state unchanged until assigned Lecturer opens; RBAC enforced independently of BR-01 business-rule evaluation.

cases.title: Student export denied and cross-scope Lecturer cannot access non-assigned session roster
preconditions: Scheduled session exists in section A assigned to Lecturer-A.
Lecturer-B is authenticated but not assigned to section A.
Student account is authenticated.
BR-18 scopes export to actor role; BR-19 denies unauthorized report access.
steps: Student calls POST /v1/exports/attendance with filters targeting section A.
Assert HTTP 403 Forbidden without partial export data leakage.
Lecturer-B calls GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance for section A session.
Assert HTTP 403 OutOfScope or Forbidden.
Lecturer-A calls the same attendance endpoint as scoped positive control.
cases.expected: Student institution-wide export denied per BR-18; cross-scope Lecturer denied roster read per BR-19 without data leakage; assigned Lecturer succeeds.

cases.title: Idempotent replay on non-Open session returns consistent SessionNotOpen not DuplicateCheckIn
preconditions: Scheduled session exists with enrolled student.
Test harness can replay POST /v1/check-ins with identical Idempotency-Key, actor, and token.
Student has no successful AttendanceRecord for this session.
steps: Submit first POST /v1/check-ins against Scheduled session; record SessionNotOpen outcome.
Replay identical request with same Idempotency-Key immediately.
Submit parallel duplicate requests with same key from harness.
Query check_in_attempts and attendance_records for student-session.
Confirm no Present/Late success row exists.
cases.expected: All replays return deterministic SessionNotOpen per idempotency rules §7.1; BR-07 DuplicateCheckIn is not evaluated because BR-01 short-circuits first; no success attendance record created.

cases.title: Alternate path: student deep-link check-in before lecturer opens returns not-open feedback then succeeds after open
preconditions: Scheduled session exists; lecturer has not yet opened attendance.
Student opens check-in URL from QR scan or deep link with token preserved.
Student is authenticated and enrolled.
steps: Student submits check-in while session is still Scheduled.
Observe SessionNotOpen rejection with Vietnamese guidance.
Lecturer opens session from assigned scope.
Student re-submits check-in with refreshed valid QR token.
Verify second attempt is not rejected with SessionNotOpen.
cases.expected: Pre-open branch fails with BR-01 SessionNotOpen and instructs student; post-open branch proceeds past session-state gate to downstream validation.

cases.title: Student mobile UI shows Vietnamese not-open error when submitting check-in before session opens
preconditions: Class session is Scheduled; lecturer has not opened attendance.
Student is logged in on mobile browser profile (iOS Safari or Android Chrome).
Student navigates to check-in page via QR scan or deep link with token in URL.
steps: Open student check-in form and confirm session context is displayed.
Attempt to submit check-in while session remains Scheduled.
Observe error banner or inline message in Vietnamese explaining attendance is not yet open.
Verify success confirmation UI is not shown and submit may be blocked or retriable.
After lecturer opens session in separate browser context, refresh or rescan and confirm not-open error no longer appears on valid submission path.
cases.expected: Browser journey surfaces BR-01 rejection with localized Vietnamese copy (SessionNotOpen equivalent); no false success state; after session opens, not-open gating is removed.
